name: Slack
description: Team messaging and collaboration platform. Yields evidence of retention and external-sharing policy, app governance, membership and access reports, and (on Enterprise Grid) organisation-wide audit logs.

steps.action: Navigate to Workspace Settings (click your workspace name > Tools & settings > Workspace settings) and screenshot the Message & File Retention configuration
steps.audit_note: Capture the full browser window so the workspace URL and date are visible in the screenshot

steps.action: In Workspace Settings, open the Permissions tab and screenshot the Slack Connect settings governing external channel and DM invitations
steps.permissions_required: Workspace Owner (Org Owner for organisation-level Slack Connect policies)
steps.audit_note: External sharing policy evidence should show who may send and accept Slack Connect invitations

steps.action: Navigate to Admin > Apps and workflows > App Management Settings and capture the app approval policy and the list of approved/restricted apps
steps.audit_note: Demonstrates that third-party app installation is governed by an approval process

steps.action: Open the analytics dashboard (Tools & settings > Workspace analytics), select the Members tab and click Export CSV for a member and activity report
steps.audit_note: The CSV includes account type, activity and deactivation status — useful for access review evidence

steps.action: On Enterprise Grid, open the organisation admin dashboard > Security > Audit Logs, filter by date range and export administrative and sign-in events
steps.audit_note: Audit logs are only available on the Enterprise Grid plan; entries are timestamped by Slack and read-only

steps.action: Upload the screenshots and CSV exports to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Review exports for message content, personal data or tokens before uploading; redact where required

steps.action: Navigate to Workspace Settings > Import/Export Data (Admin > Workspace settings, then the Import & export data option)
On Business+ or Enterprise Grid, choose Schedule Exports and set the frequency (daily, weekly or monthly) to match your evidence collection cadence
steps.permissions_required: Workspace Owner (org-wide exports on Enterprise Grid require Org Owner approval)
steps.audit_note: Each scheduled export covers only the period since the previous run — keep the schedule unbroken to avoid evidence gaps

steps.action: When each export completes, download the ZIP archive from the notification link and file it in the evidence repository
steps.security_note: Export archives can contain message content; restrict the download link and store per your data classification policy
steps.audit_note: Slack timestamps each export job; retain the completion notification alongside the archive

steps.action: Screenshot the configured export schedule itself as standing evidence that collection is automated
steps.audit_note: The schedule configuration demonstrates a repeatable, defined collection process

steps.action: Supplement with a recurring monthly Export CSV from the analytics dashboard Members tab, as Slack does not schedule member reports automatically
Create a Slack app at api.slack.com/apps requesting the auditlogs:read user scope, then have an Org Owner install it at the organisation level (not a single workspace)
steps.security_note: Store the resulting OAuth token in a secrets manager and treat it as a privileged credential; rotate on personnel change
steps.audit_note: The Audit Logs API is only available on Enterprise Grid — on other plans use scheduled exports (L2) instead

steps.action: Configure a scheduled script to call GET https://api.slack.com/audit/v1/logs with oldest/latest timestamps and action filters (e.g. user_login, app_approved, pref.retention_changed)
steps.permissions_required: Org-level OAuth token with auditlogs:read scope

steps.action: Collect the member list on any plan via GET https://slack.com/api/users.list (users:read scope) for automated access review evidence
steps.permissions_required: Bot or user token with users:read scope

steps.action: Schedule the collection daily via cron or a CI/CD pipeline, writing date-stamped output to the evidence repository
steps.security_note: Never embed tokens in scripts or config files; inject them at runtime from the secrets manager

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting so failed runs (HTTP errors, rate limits, empty responses) page the evidence owner
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should trigger re-collection within your defined SLA and be recorded

steps.action: Validate each run against a baseline event volume and alert if daily counts drop unexpectedly
steps.audit_note: Volume validation provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: Slack Managed Evidence Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: Feed Slack audit events continuously into your SIEM: Slack has no native webhook push for audit logs, so use your SIEM's Slack Audit connector (Splunk, Datadog, Google SecOps) or near-real-time polling of GET https://api.slack.com/audit/v1/logs every 5-15 minutes with cursor pagination
steps.permissions_required: Org Owner to authorise the connector (Enterprise Grid)
steps.security_note: Grant the connector only the auditlogs:read scope and restrict its token to the SIEM integration
steps.audit_note: Be honest in audit narratives that collection is high-frequency polling, not vendor push

steps.action: Track collection completeness metrics: events per day per workspace, ingest latency, and coverage across all workspaces in the organisation
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are key evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Implement automated quality checks — deduplicate on the audit event id field, detect time-series gaps between polling windows, and validate schema consistency
Build a collection-health dashboard showing events/day, p95 ingest latency, workspace coverage and failed polling runs
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as continuous evidence of process health

steps.action: Alert on collection gaps (no events received within an expected window) and on scheduled export failures so gaps are remediated within SLA
Generate an automated quarterly collection-health report covering volumes, gaps and SLA breaches, and file it as evidence of continuous monitoring
